feedback.text: Dear decision-makers, I would like to express my support for the STOP KILLING Games Directive. I think it should be a fundamental right to keep digital games purchased on my own money even if they are no longer available in online shops or when servers are shut down. I have often paid for a lot of money for such a game, and I have not just bought products. For me, these games are not a simple fun, but personal stories, relationships, development and often hundreds of hours of happy time, up to thousands of hours. Just as I can keep a book or film that I bought, I would like to have the right to access my digital games in the long term. I therefore call for the EU to grant real rights to digital customers and to prevent the practice of removing games from the digital space overnight. Thank you for your attention and trust that in the future digital property will be real property. Thank Viktor Bálint, Hungary, Budapest 2025-AUG-04
